title: Resources for Psychologists & Psychiatrists | Psychology Direct
description: Free resources for independent practitioners. CV guides, report templates, compliance documents, fee information, and professional development resources.

blocks.headline: Build and Grow Your Professional Practice
blocks.subtitle: CV guides, report templates, compliance documents, fee guidance, and professional development resources. Free resources to build and grow your practice.

cards.title: Practice Management
cards.description: CV guides, business planning, fee-setting, and growing your independent practice.

cards.title: Report Templates
cards.description: Medico-legal report frameworks, declaration templates, and formatting guidance.

cards.title: Compliance & Data Protection
cards.description: GDPR, DPA, ICO registration, professional indemnity, and legal obligations.

cards.title: Fee & Payment Guidance
cards.description: Setting rates, negotiating fees, Legal Aid rates, and billing best practices.

cards.title: Professional Development
cards.description: CPD guidance, specialisation building, case management skills, and career planning.

cards.title: Getting Work
cards.description: Marketing your practice, partnering with agencies, and building referral relationships.

children.text: Building and growing your independent practice:
How to Write an Effective CV — What to include, formatting for agencies and solicitors
Building Your Expert Witness Profile — Creating an online presence and marketing to solicitors
Business Planning for Independent Practitioners — Setting business goals and financial planning
Professional Indemnity Insurance — Why it's essential and what coverage you need
Practice Administration & Systems — Client management, scheduling, and invoicing
Build a professional practice, market yourself effectively, and manage administration efficiently.
Ready-to-use report templates:
Psychology Medico-Legal Report Template — Structure and content guidance
Psychiatry Report Template — Diagnostic assessment frameworks, ICD-10 and DSM-5
Expert Witness Declaration — MOJ standard declaration and how to incorporate into reports
Report Editing Checklist — What to review before submitting
Report Writing Best Practices — Clarity, completeness, and meeting court standards
Write professional, court-ready reports that meet quality standards.
Meeting legal and professional obligations:
Data Protection Act & GDPR Compliance — Legal requirements, patient rights, and data security
ICO Registration for Psychologists — What registration means and the application process
Regulatory Compliance — HCPC and GMC registration, CPD requirements, fitness to practise
Expert Witness Declarations & Obligations — MOJ standard declarations, duty to the court
Confidentiality & Privilege — Legal privilege, when to disclose, client consent requirements
Stay compliant, protect your clients, and understand your professional obligations.
Getting paid fairly and managing finances:
Expert Witness Fees — The Facts — What to charge and how to set rates
Understanding Legal Aid Rates — Current LAA rates for psychology and psychiatry
Fee Negotiation & Agreements — Negotiating with solicitors and standard agreement terms
Billing & Invoice Best Practices — What to include and timely billing
Managing Cash Flow in Private Practice — Forecasting income and tax planning
Get paid fairly, manage invoicing, and maintain healthy cash flow.
Building expertise and advancing your career:
Continuing Professional Development — CPD requirements for HCPC and GMC
Building Your Specialism — Developing expertise, training, and attracting specialist work
Advanced Skills in Expert Witness Work — Court preparation, cross-examination, complex cases
Supervision & Peer Review — Getting supervision in private practice
Managing Vicarious Trauma & Burnout — Self-care strategies and building resilience
Develop expertise, maintain standards, and build a sustainable, fulfilling career.
Building referral relationships and growing your workload:
How to Find Expert Witness Work — Agencies, referral networks, and direct relationships
Partnering with Psychology Direct — How our network works and benefits of joining
Networking & Relationship Building — Events, conferences, and building direct relationships
Building Your Online Presence — Website, LinkedIn, directory listings
Tender Processes & Competitive Bidding — Responding to LA and NHS tenders
Build a steady referral pipeline, grow your network, and achieve consistent workload.

blocks.heading: Interested in Joining Our Network?
blocks.bodyText: We work with 1,000+ psychologists and psychiatrists nationwide. You'll access steady referrals and handle admin-free instruction. Build a sustainable practice with us.

faqs.question: Can I download all these resources at once?
faqs.answer: You can download individual guides via the tabs above. For a complete package of all professional resources, email professionals@psychologydirect.co.uk. We'll send a ZIP file.

faqs.question: Who has written these guides?
faqs.answer: All guides are written by experienced independent practitioners and senior clinicians with 15+ years' experience in private practice.

faqs.question: Are these resources updated regularly?
faqs.answer: Yes. We update guides annually and immediately when regulations change, including Legal Aid rates, GDPR updates, and HCPC requirements. Subscribe to our professional newsletter to get notified.

faqs.question: Can I share these resources with colleagues?
faqs.answer: Yes. Share freely with colleagues in your professional network. We ask that you credit Psychology Direct, but we don't require permission.

faqs.question: I'm interested in joining Psychology Direct. Where do I start?
faqs.answer: Visit /about/careers/ for application information. Or email professionals@psychologydirect.co.uk. We'll send you a joining information pack.

faqs.question: What are the current Legal Aid rates?
faqs.answer: See the Legal Aid Rates Summary in the Fees tab. Rates are updated annually. Subscribe to our newsletter for updates.

faqs.question: I need guidance on a specific professional topic not listed. Can you help?
faqs.answer: Email professionals@psychologydirect.co.uk with your question. We create resources based on practitioner needs and may add it to the library.

faqs.question: Are these resources suitable for psychologists and psychiatrists?
faqs.answer: Most resources apply to both disciplines. Some sections are discipline-specific, for example psychology report templates differ from psychiatry ones. Check the relevant tabs.

blocks.heading: Ready to Grow Your Practice?
blocks.bodyText: Whether you're just starting or looking to expand, our resources support every stage. If you're interested in joining our network, we'd love to talk.
